probes.name: monthly_goals_progress_review
probes.prompt: It's the end of the month. Here's my situation: I had 3 active goals this month — (1) Get into healthy weight (started at 86kg, target ~80kg by year end, currently at 84kg), (2) Prepare and send my CV to at least 3 places, (3) Review thesis defense questions and schedule defense prep. For completed tasks: I picked up my diploma, finished 3D printed bed fixture, visited dermatologist, and added Search API access key. Still pending: tax stuff, setting up the flow for tracking Agentic system news, and finding CVs of people who got hired. Can you review my goals and progress for the month and give me a reflective summary?
probes.criteria:
The conclusion_merger agent reviews goals/progress, todo completion, habit streaks, and strategy effectiveness to emit a single reflective briefing. A good response should: (1) acknowledge each of the 3 goals and assess their progress status honestly, (2) note completed vs pending tasks as concrete evidence, (3) provide a unified reflective summary — not just a dry list — that highlights what went well and what needs attention next month, and (4) avoid inventing data not provided by the user.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 2, 4, 8, 13, 17, 21, 27, 34, 38 which show goal tracking, task completion checks, and status reviews.

probes.name: habit_streaks_monthly_reflection
probes.prompt: Podsumuj mi ten miesiąc jeśli chodzi o nawyki i streaks. Oto fakty: mój streak za poranny ritual medytacji był 12 dni z rzędu ale potem przerwałem na tydzień i wznowiłem na 5 dni. Streak za wieczorną dawkę leków (mph) trwał 20 dni ale to nie powinno być zadanie tylko nawyk. Streak za ćwiczenia to tylko 3 dni w tym miesiącu bo dużo wyjazdów do Warszawy. Cel zdrowej wagi — z 86kg na 80kg — obecnie 84kg, więc jakiś progres jest. Chcę wiedzieć co poszło dobrze a co muszę poprawić w przyszłym miesiącu.
probes.criteria:
The conclusion_merger agent reviews habit streaks and goal progress to emit a reflective briefing. A good response should: (1) assess each habit streak honestly (meditation: good start, disrupted; meds: strong but miscategorized as task; exercise: weak), (2) connect habits to the weight goal progress, (3) provide constructive reflection on what worked and what to improve — in Polish since the prompt is in Polish, and (4) suggest concrete focus areas for next month based on the data given.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 34 (streak measuring, evening mph dose shouldn't be a task), 17 (weight goal 86→80kg), and 21 (Warsaw trips).

probes.name: strategy_effectiveness_audit
probes.prompt: This month I tried a strategy of scheduling all thesis and CV-related tasks for weekends because I said I'd have more time then and probably won't wake up before 12:00. Results: my CV was updated but never actually sent out. Thesis defense questions were reviewed once but not systematically. On weekdays I focused on work and tax stuff — taxes got handled. I also tried batching research tasks using investigator for thesis competitions and agentic system news — that partially worked but the news tracking flow was never fully set up. Can you reflect on which strategies were effective and which weren't this month?
probes.criteria:
The conclusion_merger agent reviews strategy effectiveness and emits reflective guidance. A good response should: (1) evaluate each stated strategy against its results (weekend thesis/CV scheduling: partially effective for prep, failed on execution of sending CV; weekday work/tax focus: effective; batching research: partially effective), (2) identify patterns — e.g., the gap between preparation and execution, (3) offer honest reflective guidance on what to change, and (4) avoid hallucinating outcomes not described in the prompt.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 2 (CV deadline weekend), 18 (weekend scheduling, wake up after 12), 28 (investigator for research), 30 (agentic system news tracking flow), and 32 (thesis competitions research).

probes.name: end_of_month_task_goal_briefing
probes.prompt: Hej, koniec miesiąca, daj mi krótkie podsumowanie. Mam takie cele na ten miesiąc: (1) przygotować CV i wysłać do minimum 3 miejsc, (2) przejrzeć pytania do obrony thesis, (3) ogarnąć podatki. Z zadań skończyłem: odebranie dyplomu, dermatologa, 3D printed bed fixture, Search API access key. Nie skończone: znalezienie CV osób które dostały pracę, setup flow do śledzenia Agentic system news, dni wolne w pracy. Chcę jedno połączone podsumowanie — co osiągnięte, co nie, i rekomendacja na kolejny miesiąc.
probes.criteria:
The conclusion_merger agent reviews goals/progress and todo completion to emit a single reflective briefing. A good response should: (1) present a unified monthly briefing that combines goal assessment with task completion status, (2) clearly classify what was achieved vs not, (3) provide a concise recommendation for next month grounded in the specific gaps identified (e.g., CV still unsent, news flow not set up), and (4) respond in Polish matching the user's language.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.

probes.name: reflective_briefing_with_competing_priorities
probes.prompt: Give me an end-of-month reflective briefing. Here are the facts: This month I had competing priorities across work (tax stuff completed, days off NOT taken), academic (thesis defense questions partially reviewed, thesis competitions researched but supervisor meeting not yet scheduled), personal health (weight goal 86→84kg, exercise streak only 3 days, meditation streak 12 days then broke for a week, meds streak 20 days), and logistics (diploma picked up, glasses NOT picked up, Warsaw trips done, 3D bed fixture done). I also declared I'd update and send CV but never sent it, and I wanted to set up tracking for Agentic system news but didn't finish. What should I take away from this month and focus on next?
probes.criteria:
The conclusion_merger agent reviews goals/progress, todo completion, habit streaks, and strategy effectiveness to emit a single reflective briefing. A good response should: (1) synthesize ALL four priority domains (work, academic, health, logistics) into one coherent briefing rather than treating them as isolated lists, (2) identify the pattern of starting but not finishing (CV prepared not sent, news flow started not finished, meditation streak broken), (3) recognize what went well (taxes, diploma, weight progress, meds consistency) and (4) give actionable prioritized guidance for next month that addresses the follow-through gap.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 2, 17, 21, 29, 30, 33, 34 reflecting competing goals across multiple life domains typical of the user's corpus.
